registry.description: Submission is the voluntary placing of oneself under the authority of another — not enforced compliance but the willing acknowledgement of a proper ordering of relationship. The Greek vocabulary here is precise: to arrange oneself under, the military and relational sense of taking one's proper place in a structure of authority. Submission in Scripture is always contextual: it flows from love and wisdom rather than from domination. The supreme model is Christ's submission to the Father, which is the ground and example of all other submission.

terms.medium_def:
obedience, submission 
subordination, 1Tim. 3:4; submissiveness, obedience, 2Cor. 9:13; Gal. 2:5; 1Tim. 2:11*
terms.lsj_entry:
ὑποτᾰγή, ἡ, 
subordination, subjection, [NT+1st c.BC+]; ἐν ὑποταγῇ in a subordinate position, [Refs 3rd c.AD+]
__2 post-position, ἐν ὑποταγῇ [Refs 2nd c.AD+]
__3 copy, ψηφισμάτων.. καὶ ἐπιστολῆς [Refs 2nd c.AD+]

terms.medium_def:
to put in subjection, subject, subordinate; (passive) to submit, be subject to 
to place, or arrange under; to subordinate, subject 1Cor. 15:27; to bring under influence, Rom. 8:20; passive to be subordinated, 1Cor. 14:32; to be brought under a state or influence, Rom. 8:20; middle to submit one's self, render obedience, be submissive, Lk. 2:51; 10:17
terms.lsj_entry:
ὑποτάσσω, 
Attic dialect ὑποτάττω, passive, future ὑποτᾰγήσομαι [Refs 1st c.AD+]aorist 2 ὑπετάγην [ᾰ] [Refs 5th c.BC+], etc.:—place or arrange under, assign, τινί τι [Refs 2nd c.BC+] —passive, τὸ ὑποτεταγμένον (i.e. ὀστέον) the inferior bone, i. e. the ulna, [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__II post in the shelter of, ὑποτάσσεσθαι τινι [Refs 2nd c.AD+]; draw up behind, [Refs 2nd c.AD+]
__II.2 subject, ἑαυτοὺς οὐδενί [Refs 5th c.BC+]; subdue, make subject, Θηβαΐδα [Refs 1st c.BC+]; ἔθνη [NT+2nd c.AD+]:—passive, to be obedient, τινι [NT+1st c.AD+]; ὑποτάξονται they will submit, [Refs 1st c.AD+]: absolutely, κοὐχ ὑποτᾰγεὶς ἐβάδιζεν ὥσπερ Νικίας dejectedly, timidly, [Refs 5th c.BC+] (assuming variant); οἱ ὑποταττόμενοι subjects, [Refs 2nd c.BC+], etc.; ὑποτεταγμένοι subordinates, [Refs 1st c.BC+]; ὑποτεταγμέναι ἀρεταί subordinate virtues, i. e. the sub-divisions of the four cardinal (πρῶται) virtues, [Refs]
__II.3 passive, with dative, underlie, to be implied in or associated with, τὰ -τεταγμένα τοῖς φθόγγοις [Refs 4th c.BC+]; τὰ -τεταγμένα, ἡ -τεταγμένη διάνοια, of the content or meaning which underlies a writer's words, [Refs 1st c.BC+]
__III put after, [Refs 1st c.AD+]; subjoin, append, ὑποτετάχαμέν σοι.. τὸ ἀντίγραφον [Refs 2nd c.BC+] —passive, τὰ -τεταγμένα what follows, [Refs 2nd c.AD+]; οἱ -τεταγμένοι [ἀριθμοί] the numbers that follow, [Refs 1st c.AD+]; οἱ ὑποτεταγμένοι the following persons, [Refs 3rd c.AD+]; κῶμαι αἱ ὑποτεταγμέναι the following villages, [Refs 2nd c.AD+]
__III.2 take as a minor premiss, [Refs 2nd c.AD+]
__IV govern the subjunctive, [Refs]
